annotations.note: Heuristic 'definition' of δ as a function; no such pointwise object is captured in Mathlib.
Mathlib expresses 'total mass 1' via the IsProbabilityMeasure instance for dirac, not as this heuristic integral.
Historical Fourier integral expression by Fourier; not a standalone Mathlib lemma.
Historical heuristic representation of δ; not formalized as a Mathlib decl.
Cauchy's historical exponential form of the Fourier inversion; not a standalone Mathlib statement.
Historical chain of formal manipulations recovering δ; not formalized.
Heuristic Fourier representation of δ as an oscillatory integral; not formalized.
Heuristic pointwise description of δ; not formalized.
Expressed in Mathlib by dirac being a probability measure, not as this heuristic Lebesgue integral.
This is the sifting property for the Dirac measure, ∫ f ∂(dirac a) = f a.
The article explicitly states no such function exists; this heuristic equation is not formalized.
Heaviside step function is not present as a standalone declaration in Mathlib.
Identifies CDF of δ with Heaviside; not formalized as a standalone Mathlib statement.

Identifies δ as distributional derivative of Heaviside; not a Mathlib decl.
Heuristic integration by parts; not a formalized statement.
Stieltjes integration by parts version; not formalized as a standalone Mathlib lemma.
Same sifting identity in higher dimension; integral_dirac is stated in any measurable space.
dirac_apply' gives dirac a s = s.indicator 1 a, capturing the 0/1 case-split.

anchor.value: {\displaystyle g(s)=\operatorname {Re} \left[{\frac {-s^{k}\log(-is)}{k!(2\pi i)^{n}}}\right]={\begin{cases}{\frac {|s|^{k}}{4k!(2\pi i)^{n-1}}}&n{\text{ odd}}\\[5pt]-{\frac {|s|^{k}\log |s|}{k!(2\pi i)^{n}}}&n{\text{ even.}}\end{cases}}}
